# WOS Kernel Specification v1.0 (LLM Reference)

Source spec: `../work-spec/specs/kernel/spec.md`
Source schema: `../work-spec/schemas/wos-workflow.schema.json`

## Bottom Line Up Front

_No BLUF configured._

## Critical Schema Fields

| Pointer | Required | Type | Guidance | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `#/$defs/Transition/properties/actor` | no | string | Who acts on the transition — load-bearing for authority decisions, signature coverage (WOS-SIG-COVER-001), and audit traceability | Actor identifier responsible for performing this transition. MUST match an entry in the workflow's `actors[].id` and MUST use the same lexical pattern as `actors[].id` (ASCII letter first, then letters, digits, `.`, `_`, `-`). Single-actor transitions name one actor; multi-actor (signature-gated) transitions use the `actors` array (lint WOS-SIG-COVER-001 enforces signer coverage). Load-bearing for authority decisions and signature workflow correctness. |
| `#/$defs/Transition/properties/guard` | no | composite | Where every conditional logic decision lives — accepts FEL-string (highest-traffic FEL surface) or DecisionTableGuard (structured table form per §4.5.1), lint-checked for syntax / cross-state / function-set / boolean-shape / table-row-coverage / hit-policy correctness | Either a FEL expression (string form) or a structured decision-table reference (object form) per Kernel §4.5.1. The object form lets authors keep table-shaped decision logic as a first-class structure through the substrate without flattening to chained FEL. Round-tripping is lossless. Both forms produce a boolean used by §4.6 transition resolution; both are stateless. |
| `#/properties/$wosWorkflow` | yes | string | Document type marker and version pin for WOS Workflow documents per ADR 0076 | Sole top-level version marker that identifies this object as a WOS Workflow Document and pins it to the 1.0 line of the merged-schema product-tier model (ADR 0076). Stream identity (governance, agents, signature, custody, advanced) is implicit in the envelope version; compliance claims compose as $wosWorkflow@X.Y. Replaces the historical four-document markers $wosKernel / $wosWorkflowGovernance / $wosAIIntegration / $wosAdvanced. |
| `#/properties/actors` | yes | array | Who can act in this workflow — humans, systems, or agents (the agent type is gated by AI Integration block presence per ADR 0076 D-2; cross-ref enforced by lint WOS-AGENT-XREF-001) | Non-empty list of actors (humans, systems, or agents) that may perform transitions. Each entry has a unique `id`; transitions reference these ids. When any actor uses type `agent`, the `agents` and `aiOversight` blocks are required (lint WOS-AGENT-XREF-001). |
| `#/properties/caseFile` | no | $ref | Structured case data shape — either inline field declaration or Formspec contract reference | Structured case data for instances of this workflow: either inline `fields` (FieldDeclaration map) or a pinned `contractRef` (Formspec Definition recommended). Mutually exclusive shapes per CaseFile oneOf. |
| `#/properties/impactLevel` | yes | enum | Proportionality index that governs the strength of required controls across all embedded blocks | Consequence level classification for decisions made within this workflow (Kernel §6). `rights-impacting`: decisions affect individual legal rights — full due process REQUIRED (governance block MUST be present). `safety-impacting`: decisions affect safety — full due process REQUIRED (governance block MUST be present). `operational`: organizational operations — governance RECOMMENDED. `informational`: no binding decisions — governance OPTIONAL. Drives conditional block requirements via the schema's allOf rules. |
| `#/properties/lifecycle` | yes | $ref | State machine spine — initial state + lifecycle topology that all other concerns attach to | Author-time state machine: `initialState` plus a map of state identifiers to State declarations (atomic, compound, parallel, foreach, or final). Topology is immutable for a pinned (url, version); runtime instances follow this graph. |
| `#/properties/url` | yes | string | Stable identifier of the workflow across versions; sidecars join here | Canonical URI identifier for this Workflow Document. The URL names the workflow definition, not any particular instance — all versions of the same workflow SHOULD share this URL, and the pair (url, version) SHOULD be globally unique. Sidecars (`wos-delivery`, `wos-ontology-alignment`) join by this URL via `targetWorkflow`. Prefer stable, resolvable URIs under a namespace the authoring organization controls. |

## Behavioral Essentials

- Kernel documents define the immutable topology (states and transitions) and case state of a workflow orchestration substrate.
- The case file structure SHOULD mirror the data-capture definitions used for input in the workflow.
- Transition logic must be deterministic; choice states MUST have exhaustive, mutually exclusive conditions.
- Impact level classification established in the kernel determines baseline governance constraints for the entire workflow lifecycle.

## Conformance Essentials

- A conforming author-time workflow document must include `$wosWorkflow: "1.0"` plus required core fields `url`, `version`, `title`, `impactLevel`, `actors`, and `lifecycle` (merged envelope per ADR 0076).
- Processors must reject workflow documents that do not conform to the workflow schema shape.
- The (url, version) pair must be treated as immutable identity for pinning and execution.
